summary: Iteration 11 — validated Brand Kit feature end-to-end (backend + frontend + integration). Backend: 7/7 pytest tests PASSED (auth gating on GET/PUT, default kit shape, custom kit round-trip persistence, extra-field rejection, section CRUD round-trip preserves brand-flavored config for content + products). Frontend: brand-kit-link button on Dashboard routes to /brand; /brand page hydrates from /api/brand-kit on mount; 4 ColorField inputs (brand-{primary,secondary,text,background}-input) accept hex + enable Save; FontField selects expose all 12 expected fonts (Poppins, Inter, Roboto, Open Sans, Lato, Montserrat, Source Sans 3, Nunito, Raleway, Work Sans, Playfair Display, Merriweather); live preview updates colors/fonts live (preview h2 uses primary_color + heading_font, p uses text_color + body_font, CTA pill uses primary_color background). Integration: PASS — creating a new Content Block after seeding kit{primary:#aa2266, body:#111111, bg:#fafafa, font:Playfair Display} produced section.config.headingColor=#aa2266, bodyColor=#111111, background=#fafafa, primaryColor=#aa2266, font='Playfair Display'. Snippet font substitution PASS — toggling generated snippet on the new section showed family=Playfair+Display + font-family:"Playfair Display" with NO lingering Poppins import or font-family. Apply-brand-kit Palette button PASS — after switching kit to {#0066cc, Inter} and clicking the new apply-brand-kit testid in the editor sidebar, the section's headingColor, bodyColor, background, font all overlaid to the new brand kit values.
action_items: (optional) The Reset to defaults button is window.confirm-gated — fine, but consider replacing with a shadcn AlertDialog for consistency with the rest of the editor's confirms.

(optional) On the BrandKit page, when the user has unsaved draft changes, navigating away (e.g. the back-arrow) silently discards them — there's no 'unsaved changes' guard. Add a beforeunload / Link click warning if dirty.

(optional) The brand-kit page's preview iframe-less render uses the user's OS font for headings until Google Fonts loads — there is a small FOUT. Acceptable, but for polish consider rendering a transparent placeholder until the link onload fires.

(optional) The font option dropdown items expose data-testid like 'brand-heading-font-option-source-sans-3' — but the Radix Portal renders them outside the trigger, so multiple-instance testid collisions could occur if two FontFields ever opened simultaneously. Today each Select closes on selection, so this is theoretical only.

(nit) brandKit.js FIELD_MAP is hardcoded for 9 section types; per the brief, sections without a registered mapper just get a font field stamped on. This is documented in the function body but not in the section registry — consider colocating brand-mapping in each section's defaults file (e.g. content.js exports applyBrand) to avoid drift between section schema and FIELD_MAP.
critical_code_review_comments: BrandKitPage.save() catches all exceptions with a generic 'Could not save brand kit' toast and never surfaces the underlying error. Add explicit handling for 401 (re-auth) vs 4xx validation vs 5xx so the user can act on it.

Editor.resetToBrandKit() reads from `section.config.uid` to preserve the section's uid through the merge — but if `section.config` is undefined (corrupt/legacy data) this throws. Defensive: `section.config?.uid ?? makeUid()`.

applyFontToSnippet uses a global regex with String.replace; if the user picks a font whose name contains regex-special chars (none of the 12 do today, but if Source Sans 3 + variable weights are added later), the substitution could over-match. Escape the family name when building the import URL pattern.

BrandKitContext refreshes only on initial mount — if the user opens /brand in two tabs, edits in tab A, then creates a section in tab B, the second tab's BrandKitContext is stale. Consider a window 'focus' event handler that re-fetches, or expose a refresh() that sectionRail/Dashboard calls before createSection.

brand_kit.py router GET silently merges the stored kit on top of DEFAULT_KIT — that's correct for forward-compat (new fields) but for a malformed stored doc (e.g. heading_font=null), Pydantic will fail validation and the endpoint 500s. Wrap the BrandKit(**merged) in a try/except that falls back to DEFAULT_KIT on validation error.

BrandKit.jsx is 292 lines with FontField + SectionHeader inlined — readable, but extract FontField to /components since it's a self-contained primitive and could be reused on per-section font overrides later.
updated_files: /app/backend/tests/test_brand_kit.py (new pytest module — 7 tests covering auth, persistence, section round-trip)

seed_data_creation: Backend pytest creates 2 transient sections (TEST_brand_content, TEST_brand_products) and deletes them in finally blocks. Brand kit was mutated to custom values during testing and RESTORED to factory defaults at end of both backend and frontend runs. No persistent test artifacts remain.
context_for_next_testing_agent: Brand Kit feature is fully shipped and green. Backend persists per-user under user.brand_kit. FE applyBrandKit() in /app/frontend/src/lib/brandKit.js has FIELD_MAP for 9 section types — sections without a mapper just get a `font` field stamped (harmless; font substitution actually happens post-render via applyFontToSnippet). The apply-brand-kit Palette button in Editor sidebar header (next to RotateCcw reset-section) confirms via window.confirm and overlays brand fields onto the existing config while preserving content (text/images/products/links) and the section's uid. Snippet font substitution is verified end-to-end (family=<font>+ + font-family:"<font>" replaces Poppins references). Regression: existing sections still load/edit, dashboard is unaffected, all iter9/iter10 features (inline rename, Esc-close, save-as-template, ?new=1 deferred-delete) were not re-tested explicitly but the source files for those features were not modified per the review brief.
